observation_summary: The command-line parser fails to correctly merge user-provided arguments with default values when the number of provided arguments is less than the maximum allowed, resulting in missing default values in the final list.
hypothesis: The logic for retrieving and merging default values into the user-provided argument list is incomplete. It only considers defaults if the user-provided list is empty, failing to append remaining default values when the user provides some, but not all, required arguments.
prediction: The code will be updated to check if the number of provided arguments is less than the number of available default values, and if so, it will append the missing default values to the user-provided list.
experiment_rationale: The fix diff shows a rewrite of the logic that handles default values. Instead of a simple check for an empty list, it now explicitly iterates through the default values and appends them to the user-provided list if the user-provided list is shorter than the default list.
reasoning_summary: The defect is a procedural error in how the application computes the final list of arguments. It is not a missing guard (Checking) or a simple wrong constant (Assignment/Initialization), but a flawed algorithmic strategy for merging two lists (user-provided vs. defaults).

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While the fix involves conditional checks, the core issue is the logic of how to combine two data sets (user args and defaults), which is an algorithmic task.
The fix is not about setting a single variable to a correct value, but about implementing a loop to merge lists.
